# Arkham Advisory

> Senior advisory for organisations making consequential decisions in data, AI, and transformation.

Arkham Advisory provides senior advisory on data, AI, and organisational transformation. Engagements are independent, commercially neutral, and focused on consequential decisions.

## Advisory Services

- [Architecture and platform strategy](https://arkham-advisory.com/advisory/): Independent advice on platform direction, technical boundaries, and the architecture decisions that shape long-term delivery.
- [AI strategy and agentic systems](https://arkham-advisory.com/advisory/): Counsel on where AI and agentic systems create leverage, where they amplify existing risk, and what needs to be true before adoption can be trusted.
- [Technical due diligence](https://arkham-advisory.com/advisory/): Senior review of technology, delivery, architecture, and value risk before consequential commitments are made.
- [Transformation counsel](https://arkham-advisory.com/advisory/): Practical advisory support for transformation leaders navigating delivery confidence, system constraints, and the gap between stated strategy and operational reality.

## Writing

- [Batch, Streaming, and the Boundary That Actually Matters](https://arkham-times.com/journal/batch-streaming-and-the-boundary-that-actually-matters/): The real architectural decision is not how fast you process data. It is what you choose to treat as a fact, and whether the machines around it can reason about those facts without asking permission.
- [The modern data platform is becoming an operating system](https://arkham-times.com/journal/the-modern-data-platform-is-becoming-an-operating-system/): Why the data platform is no longer just infrastructure, and why its boundaries increasingly shape how organisations think, decide, and operate.
- [AI development & Engineering clarity](https://arkham-times.com/journal/ai-development-engineering-clarity/): AI does not reward motion. It rewards coherence. A field note on why faster execution makes clarity, documentation, and architecture more strategic.
